content.verdict: Belmond Maroma is a secluded jewel on Mexico's Caribbean coast, hidden at the end of a jungle road between Playa del Carmen and Tulum in the Riviera Maya. It occupies a private stretch of what is widely considered one of the most beautiful beaches in Mexico — 1.2 kilometers of white powdered sand and turquoise Caribbean water backed by protected mangrove forest and jungle. The resort's 65 rooms and villas are spread across the jungle and beachfront in a formula that balances utter seclusion with world-class amenities, and the Kinan Spa's Mayan healing rituals are among the most authentic wellness experiences in the Yucatan.
content.best_room: The Beachfront Pool Suite is the definitive Maroma room — a voluminous suite with a private plunge pool at the edge of the white sand beach, with nothing between you and the Caribbean horizon. The Casita Suites with garden pools offer more privacy and jungle immersion at a slightly more accessible price. All rooms feature hand-crafted Mexican furnishings, Talavera tile, and hand-laid stone.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Maroma Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive at Cancun International Airport and transfer 45 minutes south through the Riviera Maya to the hidden jungle road that leads to Maroma. The moment you step onto that powder-white beach and into that luminous Caribbean blue, the journey evaporates. Settle into your suite, order fresh guacamole and fresh-squeezed margaritas at the beach, and watch the Caribbean light shift as the afternoon fades. Dinner at El Restaurante with fresh Yucatan seafood and Mexican wine.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Kinan Spa & Caribbean Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Kinan Spa is rooted in the healing traditions of the ancient Maya — book the Temazcal (traditional sweat lodge) ceremony for couples, a profound ritual in a dome-shaped temascal followed by a cool plunge and traditional massage. It is unlike any spa treatment available elsewhere. Spend the afternoon on the beach, where the water is so clear you can see every grain of sand 8 feet below the surface.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tulum & Cenote Dos Ojos
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 45 minutes south to Tulum for a morning at the ancient Maya cliff-top ruins overlooking the Caribbean — one of the most dramatic archaeological sites in Mexico. Afterward, visit Cenote Dos Ojos, a network of crystal-clear underground river chambers connected by caverns where snorkeling in water of 99% visibility feels like swimming through liquid glass. Return by late afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Catamaran & Snorkel
itinerary_7_nights.description: Charter a private catamaran for a half-day offshore snorkeling expedition to the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef, the second-largest coral reef system in the world. The reefs offshore from Maroma host extraordinary marine life in crystal Caribbean water. Sail back in the afternoon with cold cervezas and fresh fruit as the resort comes back into view along its stretch of protected shore.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Mayan Cooking Class & Jungle Walk
itinerary_7_nights.description: Join the chef for a Mayan cooking class in the resort's outdoor kitchen — learning to grind achiote paste, prepare cochinita pibil, and make handmade tortillas on a traditional comal. In the afternoon, explore the resort's private jungle paths with a nature guide who identifies local plants used in Mayan traditional medicine — the same plants that appear in the Kinan Spa treatments.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sian Ka'an Biosphere Reserve
itinerary_7_nights.description: Book a private full-day excursion to Sian Ka'an — a UNESCO World Heritage biosphere reserve at the edge of the Riviera Maya, comprising 1.3 million acres of tropical forest, mangroves, lagoons, and barrier reef. Float down an ancient Mayan canal in a life jacket, spot crocodiles and roseate spoonbills, and snorkel the reef in near-complete solitude. One of the most extraordinary nature experiences in the Americas.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final sunrise walk along the entire private Maroma beach from end to end — 1.2 kilometers of white sand at dawn, when the only footprints are yours. Return for a room service breakfast in bed looking out at the Caribbean, then a slow departure and the 45-minute drive back through the jungle to Cancun Airport.

caveats: Maroma is 45 minutes from Cancun Airport and requires navigating a stretch of the busy Riviera Maya highway — allow extra time for airport transfers, as traffic can be heavy.
The resort is on Mexico's Caribbean coast, which has a hurricane season from June through October — tropical storms and reduced visibility are genuine risks from August through September.

The beach at Maroma has experienced some seaweed (sargassum) arrivals in recent years, as is the case across the Riviera Maya — the resort works hard to clear it but natural sargassum events are unpredictable.

faqs.question: Is Belmond Maroma good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Exceptional — it combines one of Mexico's finest beaches with genuinely authentic Mayan cultural experiences (the Kinan Spa temazcal ceremony, the cooking class), a deeply romantic jungle-and-sea setting, and the Belmond brand's legendary attention to service detail. Couples who want Caribbean beauty alongside cultural depth will find Maroma unmatched in the Riviera Maya.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is peak season on the Riviera Maya — dry, pleasantly warm (80–85°F), and with the clearest Caribbean water. November and May offer shoulder-season value with good weather. June through October is hurricane season; July and August can be very humid and August–September carry real tropical storm risk.

faqs.question: What is the Temazcal experience?
faqs.answer: The Temazcal is an ancient Mayan purification ritual conducted in a stone dome (temascal) heated with volcanic rocks and fragrant herbs. A traditional shaman leads the ceremony with chanting, medicinal plants, and steam cycles, followed by a cool plunge and grounding massage. It is a profound cultural and physical experience unique to Mexico and the resort's most celebrated offering.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Beachfront Pool Suites are limited and should be reserved 4–6 months ahead for peak season. The Temazcal ceremony requires advance booking through the spa — reserve it at the time of hotel reservation. Sian Ka'an private excursions and catamaran charters should be arranged before arrival through the hotel's concierge.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Belmond Maroma is not adults-only. The resort's intimate scale, jungle setting, and premium pricing naturally attract couples and adults far more than families. Children are welcome but the atmosphere is inherently romantic and conducive to a honeymoon experience.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Cancun International Airport (CUN), one of the most connected airports in Mexico with direct flights from many US cities, Europe, and Canada. The resort is approximately 45–50 minutes south by private car on the Riviera Maya highway. The hotel arranges private transfers and can also coordinate shared shuttle options for budget-conscious travelers.
